skill.name: software-engineer
skill.description: Guides safe software engineering work with small code changes, repository conventions, and clear validation steps.
skill.summary: Helps agents make focused code changes that are reviewable, testable, and aligned with existing projects.
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography twice and system reconnaissance once in SKILL.md. Manual review found only general engineering guidance at those lines, with no cryptographic code, command execution, file access, networking, or prompt injection. The static findings are false positives, and no additional semantic threats were found.

content.value_statement: Engineering agents can make broad or risky changes when project expectations are unclear. This skill keeps work focused on small patches, existing patterns, and clear validation.
actual_capabilities: Prompts agents to ask clarifying questions when requirements are ambiguous.
Directs agents to follow existing repository patterns and utilities.
Asks agents to propose focused validation steps after behavior changes.
limitations: Does not provide language-specific implementation rules.
Does not include automated tests, scripts, or command wrappers.
Depends on the agent to inspect the repository before editing.
Does not replace human review for high-risk production changes.

use_cases.title: Focused Feature Implementation
use_cases.description: Help an agent add a small feature while following local project conventions.

use_cases.title: Safe Refactoring Support
use_cases.description: Guide refactors toward small, reviewable patches with clear validation steps.

use_cases.title: Repository Maintenance
use_cases.description: Keep routine fixes aligned with existing utilities and dependency choices.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Small Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a narrow code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Fix the reported issue with the smallest practical change. Follow existing patterns and tell me how to validate it.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Before Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this request and ask up to three clarifying questions before changing code if the expected behavior is unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer wants cleanup without behavior drift.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this area while preserving behavior. Keep the patch reviewable and use existing project utilities where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Plan and Validate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task that changes user-visible behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the relevant code, implement the change, and propose a focused validation command or test that proves the behavior.

output_examples.input: Fix a bug in the checkout flow.
output_examples.output: A concise patch plan, targeted code edits, and one validation step for the checkout behavior.
A small refactor that follows current helper patterns and notes any tests that should be run.

output_examples.input: Add a new option to an existing command.
output_examples.output: A scoped implementation that reuses current command parsing and includes a focused manual or automated check.

best_practices: Provide the repository context and the specific behavior you want changed.
Ask for validation commands when the task affects behavior.
Review dependency additions carefully before accepting them.
anti_patterns: Requesting broad rewrites without clear acceptance criteria.
Skipping tests or validation after behavior changes.
Adding new libraries before checking existing utilities.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides AI agents toward small, reviewable software engineering changes with clear validation steps.

faq.question: Does it run commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill only provides behavioral guidance for an agent.

faq.question: Can it handle large refactors?
faq.answer: It can guide large work, but it favors breaking changes into smaller reviewable patches.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it add project dependencies?
faq.answer: No. It advises agents to prefer existing repository patterns and utilities before adding dependencies.

faq.question: Is this a security tool?
faq.answer: No. It is a coding workflow skill, though it discourages destructive actions unless explicitly requested.
